query: Can you suggest recent studies that have integrated prompt fine-tuning into semi-supervised learning workflows for natural language understanding tasks?
criteria: prompt fine-tuning semi-supervised learning natural language understanding
prompt-based semi-supervised learning for text classification

title.value: Prompt Tuning for Discriminative Pre-trained Language Models

authors_year.value: Yuan Yao, Bowen Dong, Ao Zhang, Zhengyan Zhang, Ruobing Xie, Zhiyuan Liu, et al. (2022)

source_or_doi.value: DOI: 10.18653/v1/2022.findings-acl.273; Source: Findings of the Association for Computational Linguistics: ACL 2022; OA: hybrid; Citations: 37

recommendation_reason.value: Recent works have shown promising results of prompt tuning in stimulating pre-trained language models (PLMs) for natural language processing (NLP) tasks.

supported_research_question.value: Recent works have shown promising results of prompt tuning in stimulating pre-trained language models (PLMs) for natural language processing (NLP) tasks.

method.value: Recent works have shown promising results of prompt tuning in stimulating pre-trained language models (PLMs) for natural language processing (NLP) tasks.

data_or_scenario.value: Recent works have shown promising results of prompt tuning in stimulating pre-trained language models (PLMs) for natural language processing (NLP) tasks.

main_conclusion.value: Recent works have shown promising results of prompt tuning in stimulating pre-trained language models (PLMs) for natural language processing (NLP) tasks.

limitations.value: However, to the best of our knowledge, existing works focus on prompt-tuning generative PLMs that are pre-trained to generate target tokens, such as BERT(Devlin et al.

relevance_strength.value: Recent works have shown promising results of prompt tuning in stimulating pre-trained language models (PLMs) for natural language processing (NLP) tasks.

title.value: SPC: Soft Prompt Construction for Cross Domain Generalization

authors_year.value: Wenbo Zhao, Arpit Gupta, Tagyoung Chung, Jing Huang (2023)

recommendation_reason.value: Recent advances in prompt tuning have proven effective as a new language modeling paradigm for various natural language understanding tasks.

supported_research_question.value: Recent advances in prompt tuning have proven effective as a new language modeling paradigm for various natural language understanding tasks.

method.value: This paper proposes a principled learning framework-soft prompt construction (SPC)-to facilitate learning domainadaptable soft prompts.

data_or_scenario.value: it is challenging to adapt the soft prompt embeddings to different domains or generalize to low-data settings

main_conclusion.value: We show SPC can improve upon SOTA for contextual query rewriting, summarization, and paraphrase detection by up to 5%, 19%, and 16%, respectively.

limitations.value: learning soft prompts itself is unstable, task-specific, and bias-prone.

relevance_strength.value: Recent advances in prompt tuning have proven effective as a new language modeling paradigm for various natural language understanding tasks.

m050_external_metadata.title: SPC: Soft Prompt Construction for Cross Domain Generalization

title_match.candidate_title: SPC: Soft Prompt Construction for Cross Domain Generalization

title.value: FPC: Fine-tuning with Prompt Curriculum for Relation Extraction

recommendation_reason.value: prompt-based fine-tuning has been proposed and attracted much attention

supported_research_question.value: under fully supervised and low-resource settings

method.value: we propose a novel method Fine-tuning with Prompt Curriculum (FPC)

data_or_scenario.value: four widely used RE benchmarks under fully supervised and low-resource settings

main_conclusion.value: FPC can significantly outperform the existing methods and obtain the new state-of-the-art results

limitations.value: However, such methods directly treat relation labels as class numbers, therefore they ignore the semantics of relation labels.

relevance_strength.value: The current classification methods for relation extraction (RE) generally utilize pre-trained language models (PLMs) and have achieved superior results.

cells.criterion: prompt-based semi-supervised learning for text classification
cells.snippet: Comprehensive experiments on text classification and question answering show that, compared with vanilla fine-tuning, DPT achieves significantly higher performance, and also prevents the unstable problem in tuning large PLMs in both full-set and low-resource settings.
